name: ptouch-cradle
description: Two-part desktop cradle + label-catch tray for Brother PT-P750W label printer. Round 3: owl direction abandoned. Quiet desk dock — function and frame, nothing more. All four cradle perimeter walls are 25mm uniform; the tall back panel is gone. Two-tier fillet schedule (r=3 utility, r=10 hero) applied throughout. The printer is the visual subject; the cradle is supporting frame.

params.cable_slot_style: U-notch in -Y wall, z=0..20mm. Wall above notch (z=20..25, 5mm × 25mm) prints as a short bridge between the side walls of the notch — well within FDM bridging tolerance.
params.render_with_host_note: Top-level OpenSCAD parameter render_with_host (default false). When true, host_object_proxy() draws a 78x152x143 box at the printer's installed position inside the pocket. STL exports MUST keep render_with_host=false (proxy excluded from print geometry); use-state PNG renders override via --param render_with_host=true.
params.fillet_schedule_note: Two tiers + two function-driven exceptions. r=3 utility on top edges of perimeter walls, all break-edges, tray edges. r=10 hero on cradle exterior corners (4x), base plate corners (4x top + 4x bottom), printer-section→shelf concave fillet on both ±X sides. r=1.5 on foot-to-plate upper blend (function-driven exception — foot bottom stays flat for first-layer adhesion). r=2 on tray scoop lip leading edge (intentional documented exception — sharp-ish to look intentional but not razor-sharp). NO chamfers anywhere. NO mid-tier radii.
params.label_drop_note: PT-P750W auto-cuts tape; labels exit at z=64-79mm desk-ref, clear front wall top at z=29mm desk-ref by 35-50mm, drop forward and down into tray.
params.tape_exit_position: lower-middle of front face, confirmed from product photography
params.usb_dc_ports_face: back (assumed, not confirmed in product photos)
params.user_orientation_note: +Y = user-front (tray slides out here, printer faces user). -Y = user-back (against-wall, cable notch). Render in user-frame per id/brief.md hero_views. PRIMARY hero is the use-state render with the printer proxy installed — the 'is the cradle quiet next to the printer?' check runs on cradle-user-front-in-use.png.

testPrintCandidates.reason: Tray exterior (103.2 mm W × 94.2 mm D × 21.6 mm H) must slide smoothly into cradle slot with 0.35 mm per-side clearance. If FDM dimensional error skews either part, tray will bind or rattle.

testPrintCandidates.feature: tray scoop angle with integrated finger-grip
testPrintCandidates.reason: Scoop face at 45° from horizontal plus a central concave finger-grip dip (2.5mm × 30mm) prints as threshold overhang. Verify slicer does not add unnecessary support and surface quality reads cleanly on the scoop + grip combined feature.

Wall above the cable notch (z=20..25, 5mm tall × 25mm wide) prints as a short bridge between the notch's side walls. 25mm span is within FDM bridging tolerance for PLA, but verify slicer does not add unnecessary supports and the bridge surface is clean.
Generous corner radius on a 25mm low wall — confirm it reads as 'quiet soft footprint' rather than 'bulbous' or 'cartoony'. Print-reviewer aesthetic check.

Quarter-arc concave sweep on ±X sides of the cradle at the printer-section→shelf transition. Hero radius bumped from r=8 to r=10 in v3. Verify the fillet reads as a deliberate sculptural move (the one sculptural move on the cradle) rather than a slicer artifact.
Tray walls at 1.6 mm (4 perimeters at 0.4 mm) are above minimum but thin. Verify wall integrity.
